recent_calls.summary: The user called to inquire if Budget would be coming out tomorrow for Melinda Wade Lemay. The agent informed the user that the schedule could not be confirmed after hours but noted the request and assured a follow-up call the next business day. The user requested a callback as soon as the team was in, and the agent confirmed the office hours and noted the request.

The user called after hours seeking an estimate for heating and cooling services. The agent confirmed the after-hours status but offered to assist in starting the process. The call ended shortly after without further details or service requests.

The user, Inisia, called after hours to report an issue where the wiring was not hooked up to the outside unit after work was done, and they had to hire another company to fix it. The agent documented the issue and informed the user that a representative would follow up on the next business day. The call ended with the user confirming no further assistance was needed.

Emily called to report a leak in her garage ceiling that may be related to plumbing or HVAC, with a risk of major damage. The agent documented the urgent request and flagged it for follow-up on the next business day, explaining that immediate dispatch was not possible after hours. Emily was informed about the after-hours process and confirmed the updated service address.

The user called after business hours requesting to speak with a representative. The agent informed the user that direct connection was not possible and offered to take their information for a callback. The call ended abruptly before the user could provide complete information.

Yolanda called to reschedule her cooling service appointment to Tuesday, anytime, due to a work commitment. The agent confirmed her contact details and service address, noted her interest in home maintenance programs, and collected positive feedback on the interaction. The agent assured Yolanda that the team would follow up on the next business day to confirm the new appointment.

The user returned a call from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing but confirmed they did not initiate contact or require any services. The agent clarified the purpose of the call and offered assistance, but the user declined any help and ended the call politely.

Deborah Cabot called to schedule a pending service for her non-working air conditioner, which she marked as an emergency due to the heat and her husband's recent surgery. The agent documented the emergency and informed Deborah that a team member would contact her on the next business day to prioritize the repair. The call ended with the agent confirming all details and no further assistance requested.

John called to check if a technician was still scheduled to come to his place. The agent informed him that live transfers were not available after 4 PM but documented his request for a callback on the next business day. John declined further assistance and the call ended with a promise of follow-up.

Kathy, an escalation specialist from American Home Shield, called to request assistance with dispatch number 42659279 and asked for confirmation on whether repair or replacement had been completed. The agent informed her that live transfers were not available after hours but documented the request for a callback on the next business day. The call ended with no further questions from Kathy.

The user, Camren Scaife, called to schedule an AC installation appointment for Saturday afternoon. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and time preference, and informed the user that the team would reach out to confirm the appointment. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided no special instructions for the technician.
The user called to be connected to the heating and cooling department. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate department.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The agent, Sophie, contacted Vicky to schedule an appointment but quickly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 18 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

Mike Pagano called to schedule a routine air conditioner checkup as a follow-up to a previous furnace service. The agent confirmed the correct address and booked an appointment for Friday, June 5th at 12 PM. The user declined to provide feedback but was satisfied with the booking process.

The user, Jonathan Hartzell, called to schedule a standard air conditioning service because it was not cooling. The agent booked an appointment for the next day at 10 AM, as no same-day slots were available. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.
The user requested to speak with Amy, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate person successfully. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user called to speak with Nicole and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate person successfully. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
The user called and asked to speak to Daniel but the call ended abruptly before the agent could assist further.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user returned a call regarding a survey and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user, Jerrica, scheduled a cooling service appointment for 8 AM the next day at her property in Fairview Heights, Illinois. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that a representative would contact her to confirm the exact time. The booking attempt encountered an error, but the agent assured that a human agent would follow up to confirm availability.

Elizabeth from American Home Shield called to follow up on an expedited dispatch assigned to Budget, seeking confirmation if they would take the dispatch. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the message and informed Elizabeth that the team would follow up, but since the time frame had passed, Elizabeth decided to transfer the request to another party. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

The user called back after missing a call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the call and facilitated the transfer without any issues.

The user, Gwen Carter, called to inquire about the status of a work order. The agent confirmed the user's details and appointment information, noted that the appointment had passed, and assured the user that the team would follow up with status information. The call ended with no further questions from the user.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live representative. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Mary from American Home Shield called Budget to inquire about the status of a dispatch related to an AC issue at a property in Saint Louis. The agent gathered the necessary details and transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.
The user requested to speak to a live representative and the agent transferred the call successfully to another agent. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called and was greeted by the agent Sophie, who then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called to inquire about billing. The agent informed the user that they could not access billing information directly and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user called and requested to speak with the billing department. The agent apologized for not having direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team successfully.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

Elizabeth from American Home Shield called to assign an expedited work order for air conditioning service at 6777 Blossom View Drive due to the homeowner's daughter having respiratory issues. The agent confirmed the details, including the urgency and dispatch number, and assured a response within thirty minutes. The user declined additional maintenance program information and provided positive feedback on the call experience.
The user requested to speak to a manager, and the agent promptly initiated a call transfer to connect the user with a live agent. The call was successfully transferred without further interaction.

The user called to inquire about service options for their ZIP code in Illinois. The agent informed the user that plumbing services are not available but HVAC and water heater services are offered. The user mentioned they already have an HVAC service scheduled, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Larry Edmonson called to inquire about a scheduled air conditioner service under his wife's name, Carol Edmondson. The agent was unable to find an appointment and offered to transfer him to a live representative, which Larry accepted. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

The user called to inquire why the service technician did not review the uploaded images before the visit. The agent apologized and attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call was successfully transferred to another agent.

The user called after missing a previous call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent acknowledged the user's situation and successfully completed the call transfer.

The user, Russell Elton, requested routine maintenance for their air conditioners, noting the upstairs unit isn't cooling properly. The agent collected necessary details, offered available time slots, and successfully booked an appointment for May 29, 2026, at 12 PM. The user declined to provide an email and declined to give feedback at the end of the call.

The user called to report a delay with their technician who was supposed to arrive over an hour ago. The agent apologized and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to schedule an appointment for cooling service and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The conversation was brief and ended with a successful call transfer. The user expressed some frustration during the call.
The user called to request an old invoice. The agent informed the user that billing information was not accessible directly and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.
The user requested to speak with customer service, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the type of Freon used in their AC unit from a previous repair. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to that information but would pass the request to the team. The user requested to speak to someone directly, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.
The user called to speak with someone about their AC. The agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent and successfully transferred the call. The call ended with a transfer to another agent.

The user called to make an appointment related to home warranty work through American Home Shield. After confirming the user's role as the owner and the nature of the service, the agent transferred the call to a live agent as requested by the user.
The user, Neelam Sharma, called and requested to speak with Nicole. The agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent as requested.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
